stories.body: The United States struck radar, port and military sites across Iran for a second straight night after President Donald Trump declared last month’s interim truce “over,” with explosions reported in Bandar Abbas, Sirik, Bushehr province and Abu Musa Island as Tehran continued retaliatory drone and missile attacks on US facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait; Central Command said the strikes aimed to secure shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, but Iranian media reported no damage to the Bushehr nuclear plant while oil prices spiked and US politicians, including Senator Bernie Sanders, warned of an escalating war that has already seen Iran hit commercial shipping and US assets around the Gulf.
stories.headline: US pounds Iranian targets again as Gulf tensions surge

stories.body: Hundreds of thousands of Iranians packed Tehran’s Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla complex for week-long funeral ceremonies honoring Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, whose 37-year rule ended when US and Israeli strikes killed him in February; state television showed his flag-draped coffin beside those of slain relatives atop a Kaaba-like platform as mourners chanted for revenge, while authorities planned processions through Qom, Najaf, Kerbala and Mashhad despite his successor, son Mojtaba Khamenei, remaining out of public view after being wounded in the same attack.
stories.headline: Mourners flood Tehran as Khamenei’s funeral rites begin

stories.body: Russia’s military told President Vladimir Putin that its southern grouping had seized Kostiantynivka, a key logistics hub in eastern Ukraine’s Sloviansk-Kramatorsk defensive belt, and was tightening control near Lyman while expanding security zones to counter intensified Ukrainian long-range strikes on Russian oil infrastructure; Kyiv offered no confirmation, but Putin used a command-post visit to warn that continued Ukrainian attacks on civilian targets would force Moscow to push deeper into neighboring territory, even as Ukrainian leaders insist Russian advances have slowed.
stories.headline: Russia says Kostiantynivka captured as Donetsk push grinds forward

stories.body: Kyiv authorities reported large plumes of smoke, burning cars and fires in a Podil non-residential compound and a nine-story apartment block after heavy Russian strikes on Tuesday, prompting officials to urge residents into shelters while President Volodymyr Zelenskiy warned of a possible massive attack and told citizens to heed air-raid alerts amid Russian threats of “systematic” hits on command centers following a deadly blast in occupied Luhansk that Ukraine denies carrying out.
stories.headline: Kyiv endures renewed missile barrages and fire outbreaks

stories.body: Scientists at the EU’s Copernicus service said Western Europe’s June temperatures averaged 3.06C above the 1991-2020 baseline while global readings made it the planet’s second-warmest June, with marine heatwaves, Barcelona hitting 40.5C, France battling deadly wildfires and the UK facing a third heatwave that is prolonging tropical nights and widespread sleep disruption, underscoring warnings from climate researchers that cumulative heat is amplifying risks to people, ecosystems and infrastructure.
stories.headline: Western Europe logs hottest June on record as heatwaves stack up

stories.body: The International Monetary Fund cut its 2026 global growth forecast to 3.0 percent, down 0.1 points from April, citing lingering energy shocks from the US-Israel war on Iran even as AI-driven investment partially offsets the drag, and warned that inflation will average 4.7 percent this year before easing; the baseline assumes the Strait of Hormuz reopens mid-July, but the latest US strikes on Iran sent Brent crude toward $79 a barrel, highlighting what IMF officials called the fragile balance between geopolitical risk and tech-fueled optimism.
stories.headline: IMF trims 2026 growth outlook as Iran war roils energy markets

stories.body: Progressive Maine Senate nominee Graham Platner suspended his campaign and said he will withdraw from the ballot after POLITICO detailed an allegation by Jenny Racicot that he forced sex on her nearly five years ago, a claim he denies but which triggered mass defections by Democratic leaders, national committees and major endorsers already uneasy over prior controversies involving his online posts and tattoo imagery; the state party now has until July 27 to pick a replacement in a convention process.
stories.headline: Maine Democrat Graham Platner quits Senate race amid assault claim

stories.body: In a 52-47 pre-dawn vote, Senate Republicans approved an additional $70 billion for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol operations, rejecting Democratic efforts to permanently kill former President Donald Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, which critics call a slush fund for allies despite Justice Department assurances it will not proceed; the House will take up the bill next week amid GOP infighting over amendments and concerns that maintaining the fund could backfire in tight reelection races.
stories.headline: Senate backs $70 billion ICE boost but leaves Trump ally fund intact

stories.body: Relatives of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a longtime Houston resident without criminal convictions who was driving construction workers when an ICE officer fatally shot him after agents said he tried to ram them with his van, called for an independent investigation at an emotional news conference where his sons displayed photos and wept beside a growing memorial; Representative Sheila Jackson Lee joined demands for transparency as advocates questioned the official account of the July 7 shooting.
stories.headline: Family of Houston man shot by ICE agent demands outside probe

stories.body: Five astronauts briefly sheltered in a docked SpaceX Crew Dragon as NASA ordered a safe-haven procedure when Roscosmos attempted to saw into Russia’s Zvezda module to reach one of two worsening air leaks that had doubled to roughly two pounds of air per day, but the agency lifted the evacuation directive after Russian engineers paused their repair plan; officials stressed there is no immediate danger to the seven crew members, though the incident underscores ongoing disagreements over how to fix persistent leaks aboard the aging station.
stories.headline: NASA rescinds ISS evacuation order after leak repair dispute pauses

stories.body: Madrid prosecutors accused Judge Juan Carlos Peinado of lacking rigor and concocting a “confabulation” to justify confiscating the passport of Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s wife, Begoña Gómez, including alleging without evidence that her police escort could facilitate a flight abroad and entertaining online rumors of Dominican citizenship, while the judge doubled down in a June 30 filing citing historical cases of guarded European leaders fleeing corruption probes—remarks that have intensified criticism of his investigation into Gómez.
stories.headline: Prosecutors say judge plotted to seize Begoña Gómez’s passport

stories.body: After Donald Trump publicly called Spaniards “bad people” and claimed to order an end to bilateral trade in retaliation for Madrid’s defense outlays, Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said he was taking the remarks with patience, noting that EU membership makes such a unilateral trade rupture impossible and that he later chatted amicably with Trump about football and golf during the NATO summit despite the US leader’s harsh rhetoric.
stories.headline: Sánchez shrugs off Trump threat to cut Spain-US trade over NATO spending

stories.body: Spain’s Supreme Court gave the government’s extraordinary migrant regularization drive a boost by refusing petitions from Aragón and Valencia to suspend the program or seek a preliminary ruling from the European Court of Justice, concluding that questions raised by three magistrates did not warrant referral and allowing the process—which has generated long lines at municipal offices—to continue for now.
stories.headline: Supreme Court declines to refer migrant regularization to EU judges

stories.body: Facing backlash after Popular Party leader Alberto Núñez Feijóo described rising medical leave as a “cancer” and questioned paying full wages to absent workers, PP finance chief Juan Bravo said the remarks targeted only fraudulent claims and conceded the party “may not have explained it well,” prompting Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez to caution that a PP government would bring austerity reminiscent of Mariano Rajoy’s era.
stories.headline: PP softens Feijóo sick-leave comments as Sánchez warns of cuts

stories.body: Geologist Albert Ventayol of the College of Geologists’ Georisk Observatory told ACN that the Barcelona L9 metro stretch where a sinkhole opened was identified years ago as a delicate zone because the tunnel-boring machine transitions from granite to softer slate and crosses old stream sediments, likely causing over-excavation that created a void which propagated to the surface, while construction leaders emphasized strict monitoring protocols and said injected concrete needs three days to set before residents return.
stories.headline: Experts say Sant Gervasi sinkhole site on L9 tunnel was long flagged as risky

stories.body: Catalonia’s MoMo mortality system recorded 97 heat-related deaths statewide between July 1 and 8, including 50 in Barcelona province—mostly women aged 85 or older—nearly matching the province’s toll for the entire month of June, as a second intense heatwave sent temperatures above 40°C, with the Fabra Observatory hitting a record 40.9°C and public health officials warning that this summer could set new mortality highs.
stories.headline: Heat blamed for 50 deaths in Barcelona province during first week of July

stories.body: Following the 2,200-hectare Gavarres wildfire, Presidency Minister Albert Dalmau and Territory Minister Sílvia Paneque promised at a Baix Empordà meeting to finance road repairs, expand water depots and speed completion of the massif’s road-network master plan alongside the Girona provincial council, while landowners prepare a restoration strategy to clear burned timber and revive farmland ahead of another joint session in 15 days to finalize costs.
stories.headline: Catalan government and Girona council pledge funds for Gavarres fire defenses

stories.body: The forest fire near Sentmenat in Vallès Occidental entered its third day without stabilization after burning about 200 hectares, with 240 Bombers de la Generalitat personnel and 70 ground and air assets focused on preventing spot fires from advancing toward the El Farell estate and Sant Sebastià de Montmajor, where some 300 people remain confined because the steep, vegetation-heavy terrain and hot, dry marinada winds keep the blaze’s flanks unstable and complicate perimeter closure.
stories.headline: Sentmenat wildfire still active as crews shield El Farell community
